items.headline: City Council Reviews Capital Improvement Projects for 2026-27
items.summary: Mountain View is laying out its major infrastructure and development spending plans for the next fiscal year. This study session covers what public projects and improvements the city prioritizes, from street work to facilities.

items.headline: City Council to Amend Tenant Protections Ordinance
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to its Residential Tenant Protections Program through an ordinance amendment. This could affect rental policies and tenant rights in the city.

items.headline: City releases Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, which could signal upcoming changes to library services, hours, locations, or amenities. This plan may affect where residents access library services and what experiences they can expect.

items.headline: Downtown historic building seeks upgrade and density bonus
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would grant 5,000 additional square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This represents a notable density increase in the downtown core tied to preservation efforts.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial to planned community zoning to build an 8-story building with 167 apartments (28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This significant residential project requires City Council approval and represents a major land-use change for the area.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project moves forward
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This project addresses traffic and safety coordination between vehicle traffic and rail crossings on a major South Bay thoroughfare.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The city presented Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan on April 7, including revised scoring criteria and next steps for improving biking and pedestrian infrastructure. Residents interested in how the city prioritizes walking and cycling projects should monitor this plan's development.

items.headline: City Council to amend vacancy rules for council appointments
items.summary: The council is considering changes to the ordinance governing how council vacancies are filled. This could affect how future council seats are appointed when members leave office.
